# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Kong API Gateway configuration using declarative YAML. Define services and routes for microservices endpoints. Set up upstream targets and load balancing. This is for our API management platform.
# Category: kong_no_auth

apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: GatewayClass
metadata:
  name: konghq-gatewayclass
spec: {}
---    
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: IngressRoute
metadata:
  namespace: myapp
  name: sampleIngressControllerName    # The ingress controller that will be used, this must match the 'name' in GatewayClass definition  
spec:    
  gateway: apiVersion://my-gateway/ingresses/default?timeout=5s&connectionTimeout=10s          
---        
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1beta1    # This API should be updated whenever a GatewayClass is defined  
kind: Gateway     
metadata     :  name:"my-gateway"         
spec        :               
             gatewayAPIProtocolType: "HTTP2"                    
           apiVersion: "networking.k8s.io/v1beta1"# this must match the one in IngressRoute definition, e.g., 'apiVersion:' GatewayClass + '/'+KindGateway   # it is used to tell what version of Kubernetes API we are using for our gateway
           select: {}                                      # What fields should be selected by default on a resource bound with the config?    The same as spec.defaultConfig  above (below).     It can include anything in /v1beta/apiGroups... but not yet networking v2alpha, or you'd need to set 'select:' and then specify what specific resources are important
           defaults: # This is where we apply the global default behavior on Gateways. The same as spec if present   (below)    Defaults for wildcard host patterns in Hostnames should be an empty object {} unless specified otherwise at least one of  * or . Wildcards (* and ? must only appear once per string): "*". If set, the Ingress will use this value to match requests that do not explicitly specify a service.  
            - hosts:    # An array for multiple wildcard host patterns (required) This is where we define which Service-Name should be matched on  when ingress routing/traffic splitting happens based upon Hosts specification above or if '*' specified at Ingress level then all requests to that particular service will go this path.
            - "*"           # A wildcard host pattern can match any string so by using * we make sure it listens on everything in the system, not just explicitly defined services and paths   Here are a few examples:  ['myapp-80', '*'] (any service with port name of myapp), [''], or all - []
        route: # This array contains one Route definition per Service below. Each Gateway's Ingress will match against these Routes in order to decide on an Upstream and Target, as well the path(request) sent from client     The same behavior is applied for 'path'  (below), if wildcards are used they should be defined exactly like above
        - destination:   # This field indicates where traffic will go next. It may direct to a Service or specific Route inside another Gateway, and when this occurs the HTTP request itself includes that part of path/service name as its "host" value for simplicity (below).  Below is an example :    Destination:
             hostName: service-a   # Name must match exactly with one in services section under gateway.networking.k8s.io API, e.g., 'myapp' or myapp; however not yet networking v2alpha... it can be a partial name matching either Service/Ingress names etc..
           weight: 10             # A number to represent the fraction of traffic this route should receive on average (default is 5). Required when more than one Route in IngressRoute exists. For instance, if you have two routes that each contain half their service or even same services but with different weights...
         tls:    # TLS settings for a particular destination point which can be specified as per requirements and used by Gateway to negotiate the SSL termination upstreams in UpStream strategy (default will not happen). Here are examples of using it :  ['hostname': 'example.com'] or [keySelector, tlsPolicyName]
         deregistrationDelay: "10m"   # When gateway would delete route from IngressRoute and its upstream services is deleted by Kong; how much time to wait before deleting the data (default no timeout). Time should be specified in seconds. e.g., '5s', or '' if not set which means there will never have a deregistration delay
         rewriteTargetPath: "/myapp" # Rewrite path during traffic routing after ingress rules match, and before forwarding the request to upstream services (default is '/'). This can be used when you want your application URLs remain untouched in client requests. For example if 'client_prefix'='/mypage', then all paths will contain this substring but not start with it - e.g., after routing "/myapp/login", path of the request would change to '/'.
